exemplar.title: misuse of power and how it affects the common people

detailedExemplarData.title: misuse of power and how it affects the common people

strengths: Generally appropriate and varied vocabulary for the topic
Clear and effective pronunciation inferred from orthography
Consistent communication effectiveness despite occasional errors
weaknesses: Frequent code-switching into English disrupts register
Noticeable grammatical lapses and typos in complex structures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student exhibits ongoing issues with code-switching and script consistency, the overall range of vocabulary, reasonable grammatical variety and inferred pronunciation clarity support placement in the top band. Their language remains largely intelligible, varied and purposefully deployed throughout the response, justifying the full 12 marks.

strengths: Presentation remains consistently relevant to the chosen extracts
Competent citation of key lines from both texts
weaknesses: Observations are mostly descriptive and lack deeper analytical depth
Support for thematic claims is uneven and occasionally superficial
Modern parallels are noted but not fully integrated with the extract analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The response demonstrates steady relevance to both Animal Farm and the Andher Nagari passage, fitting the 3â4 band. However, its development of observations remains at a competent rather than fully effective level, with limited depth and support, warranting 4 marks.

strengths: Responses are mostly relevant and appropriate to likely prompts
Good breadth of examples (Stalin, North Korea, judicial corruption)
weaknesses: Answers occasionally feel rehearsed rather than fully adaptive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conversation responses are generally appropriate and show breadth of development, placing them at the upper end of the 3â4 band. While personal engagement is limited, the student sustains relevance and appropriateness well, meriting 5 marks.

strengths: Comprehension and interaction appear mostly sustained
Consistent participation in the target language with appropriate responses
weaknesses: Reliance on prepared script reduces spontaneity
Few independent contributions beyond response to prompts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Interaction is maintained with few breakdowns, and the student provides responses that demonstrate comprehension, aligning with the upper end of the 3â4 band. The lack of fully independent contributions prevents placement in the top band, supporting a score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student uses basic structures (S-O-V statements, simple conjunctions) and attempts some complex constructions with participles and relative clauses, e.g. âà¤à¤¬ à¤¨à¥à¤¯à¤¾à¤¯ à¤µà¥à¤¯à¤µà¤¸à¥à¤¥à¤¾ à¤­à¥à¤°à¤·à¥à¤ à¤¹à¥à¤¤à¥ à¤¹à¥, à¤¤à¥ à¤¸à¤®à¤¾à¤ à¤à¤°à¤¾à¤à¤à¤¤à¤¾â¦â.  Errors of agreement, missing post-positions and inconsistent script choice appear (âà¤µà¤¹ à¤¸à¤¤à¥à¤¤à¤¾ à¤¹à¤¥à¤¿à¤¯à¤¾ à¤²à¥à¤¤à¤¾ à¤¹à¥â, âà¤¸à¤®à¤¾à¤ ko sahan karna lagta hai lâ), indicating partial control.  Variety is present but uneven; complexity does not consistently support meaning.  This aligns with the middle band rather than the âeffectively usesâ 7â9 band.

As no recording is provided, pronunciation can only be inferred from orthography.  The script is mostly Devanagari; spellings are standard enough that a listener would likely grasp intended sounds, though English insertions and inconsistent transliteration could affect flow.  This corresponds to âpronunciation generally clearâ, i.e., the 4â6 band.

Responses are provided in the target language with reasonable length, indicating participation.  However, lack of spontaneous elaboration or initiative keeps the contribution within âmostly sustainedâ rather than âsustained with independent contributionsâ, supporting a 3-point award in the 3â4 band.

The presentation constantly links back to the chosen Animal Farm excerpt and the Andher Nagari passage, framing every point around âà¤¸à¤¤à¥à¤¤à¤¾ à¤à¤¾ à¤¦à¥à¤°à¥à¤ªà¤¯à¥à¤â.  Occasional digressions to modern examples remain connected to the same issue.  Therefore relevance is âmostlyâ assured, meriting the top of the 3â4 band.

Answers (e.g., modern parallels, quotation explanations) show some breadth, referencing Stalin, North Korea, judicial corruption, but development tends to stay general.  Personal insight and layered argument are limited; hence depth is âmostly broadâ rather than âbroad and personalâ, warranting mid-band.

Based on the script, responses to likely teacher prompts appear on-topic and intelligible, yet they mainly reiterate prepared ideas about corruption without demonstrating nuanced adaptation.  Thus relevance is mostly adequate but not consistently elaborated, aligning with the 3â4 band.

Inferred from the text, the student would comprehend familiar follow-up questions and maintain dialogue, yet the heavy reliance on scripted content suggests occasional need for prompts.  Interaction seems âmostly sustainedâ rather than fully independent, situating performance in the 3â4 range.

The student cites key lines (âà¤¸à¤­à¥ à¤ªà¤¶à¥ à¤¸à¤®à¤¾à¤¨â¦â, âà¤¹à¤¾à¤¯ à¤¬à¤¾à¤ª à¤°à¥â¦â) and offers brief stylistic or thematic comments (satire, repetition, irony).  Analysis shows understanding but remains surface-level and sometimes descriptive.  Support for claims is present but not fully developed or explored in depth, fitting a âcompetentâ rather than âeffectiveâ use of the extract.

The student employs a reasonable range of everyday Hindi such as âà¤¶à¥à¤·à¤£â, âà¤à¤°à¤¾à¤à¤à¤¤à¤¾â, âà¤µà¥à¤¯à¤µà¤¸à¥à¤¥à¤¾à¤à¤¤ à¤­à¥à¤°à¤·à¥à¤à¤¾à¤à¤¾à¤°â alongside topic-specific terminology like âallegoryâ, âsystemic corruptionâ. However, the commentary frequently code-switches into English (e.g. âsystemic corruptionâ, âglobal issueâ, âtime frameâ), transliterates English words (âsiezeâ, âexplanationâ) and repeats set phrases.  Idiomatic or nuanced expressions are rare.  Overall, vocabulary is generally appropriate and shows some variation, but it does not reach the sophistication or consistency required for the 7â9 band.

Despite frequent grammatical lapses and spelling slips, the main thread of argument remains understandable.  Mis-ordered words, English insertions and typos occasionally slow the reader but rarely block comprehension.  Basic structures are largely accurate; complex ones contain noticeable mistakes.  Hence accuracy is âmostlyâ satisfactory with interference âsometimesâ occurring, matching the 4â6 band.

annotations.comment: The student introduces the famous quote without situating it within the broader argument or explaining its relevance to the theme. Adding a brief lead-in would clarify its purpose.
This narrative summary lacks analytical commentary. The student should focus on interpreting key moments rather than retelling plot events.
This section remains largely descriptive. Encourage integration of thematic analysisâhow does this scene satirize authority?
The quote ends abruptly due to an OCR cutoff. Ensure complete sentences are presented to avoid confusion.
Usage of English titles like âDaily Mirrorâ within a Hindi translation disrupts consistency. Consider transliterating or translating for uniformity.
The table from âà¤à¤à¤§à¥à¤° à¤¨à¤à¤°à¥â is presented without analytical commentary. The student should link specific dialogue to themes of injustice.
The English introduction lacks Hindi engagement and relies heavily on broad statements. Infusing targeted Hindi terms would strengthen the globalâissue framing.
This paragraph codeâswitches heavily. Aim for consistent use of one language per paragraph to improve clarity and register.
The analysis of the Animal Farm quote remains surfaceâlevel. The student should explore how Orwellâs use of paradox intensifies satire.
No direct quotes from Andher Nagari appear in this section. Incorporating key lines will substantiate thematic claims.
Modern parallels (Stalin, North Korea, Zimbabwe) are noted but lack integration with the original texts. Tie each example back to a specific passage.
The mixed use of Devanagari and Roman script (âupnyas à¤nimà¤² à¤«à¤¾à¤°à¥à¤®â) interrupts reader flow. Stick to one script per language segment.
This summary of the judicial systemâs corruption restates ideas already mentioned. Deepen by analyzing Harishchandraâs use of absurdity as a critique.
The personal introduction section is cluttered with untranslated English and placeholders (e.g., âtime frame of Rachanaâ). Streamline and complete missing elements.
Spelling of âsiezeâ and leftover stageânote parentheses distract from the argument. Correct typos and remove drafting notes.
Frequent English inserts within Hindi sentences hinder the tonal consistency. Aim for full Hindi phrasing in analytical sections.
The statement about the novelâs core idea would benefit from citing page numbers or scene contexts to ground the analysis.
Good identification of irony, but the student could further discuss how Orwellâs syntax inversion contributes to the satirical effect.
The analysis of repeated exclamations (âà¤à¥à¤à¥à¤! à¤à¥à¤à¥à¤!â) effectively highlights emotional impact. Well done on this focused close reading.
The modern society linkage is generic. Recommend adding a concise case study or data point to substantiate this connection.
Typo in the closing sentence: the stray âlâ after âlagta haiâ should be removed to ensure grammatical accuracy.
The conclusion reiterates earlier points without adding new insight. Consider synthesizing the analysis into a more concise takeaway.
The section heading âGlobal issue connection to second workâ is not developed. Expand this to explain how the second extract specifically illustrates the global theme.
The single-word placeholder âvyakranâ indicates an incomplete grammar section. Develop this with examples of relevant structures.
